generalInfo.description: A synthetic ThML file used to exercise the Ember importer.

div1.title: Book One — Of the Inward Life

div2.title: Of the Imitation of Christ, and Contempt for the Vanities of the World
div2.p: "He that followeth me, walketh not in darkness," saith the Lord. These are the words of Christ; and they teach us how far we must imitate his life and manners, if we would be truly enlightened, and be delivered from all blindness of heart.The opening sentence echoes the prologue of the gospel.
Let, therefore, our chief endeavour be to meditate upon the life of Jesus Christ.Be ye followers of me, as I am of Christ.

lg.l: O most sweet and loving Jesus,
whom I desire with all my heart,
be present unto me; abide with me,
and let no creature satisfy me without thee.

list.item: To fear God and to keep his commandments.
To despise the world, and account it as nothing.
To rest only in our Lord and Maker.

div2.p: Therefore vanity of vanities, and all is vanity, except to love God and serve him only.Cf. Augustine, Confessions, X.27.
For Augustine writes thus: that the soul which is not anchored in eternity is tossed about by every wind.

div2.title: Of Thinking Humbly of Oneself
div2.p: Every man naturally desires knowledge; but what doth knowledge avail without the fear of God?
Surely a humble husbandman that serveth God is better than a proud philosopher who, neglecting himself, considereth the course of the heavens.
Many words satisfy not the soul; but a good life refresheth the mind, and a pure conscience yieldeth great confidence in God.

div1.title: Book Two — Admonitions to the Inward Life

div2.title: Of the Inward Conversation
div2.p: The kingdom of God is within you, saith the Lord.

Turn thee with thy whole heart unto the Lord, and forsake this miserable world; and thy soul shall find rest. Learn to despise outward things, and to give thyself to those that are inward; and thou shalt see the kingdom of God come within thee.
Christ will come unto thee, and shew thee his consolation, if thou prepare for him a worthy mansion within thee.

div2.title: Of Humble Submission
div2.p: Make no great account who is for thee, or against thee; but mind only the present, and take care that God be with thee in everything thou doest.
Have a good conscience, and God will well defend thee; for he whom God will help, no malice of man shall be able to hurt.
